---
title: "TCGA"
author: Yuan Li (yuan.li@nbis.se)
format: html
---

```
Here we will use a global sample-centric approach to estimate the correlation between CellFie tasks and KEGG pathways
```

```{r}
standadizeDFinList = function(df_list){
    # 1. Get the global union of all row names and column names
    all_rows = unique(unlist(lapply(df_list, rownames)))
    all_cols = unique(unlist(lapply(df_list, colnames)))

    # 2. Conform each data frame to the master dimensions
    standardized_list = lapply(df_list, function(df) {
        # Create an empty template filled with NA
        template = matrix(NA, 
            nrow = length(all_rows), 
            ncol = length(all_cols), 
            dimnames = list(all_rows, all_cols))
        
        # Cast to data frame if needed
        template = as.data.frame(template)
        
        # Fill the template with matching data from the original data frame
        template[rownames(df), colnames(df)] = df
        
        return(template)
    })
    return(standardized_list)
}
```

```{r}
# BiocManager::install("TCGAbiolinks")
# install.packages("ggalluvial")
# install.packages("htmlwidgets")
# install.packages("networkD3")
# install.packages("webshot")
library(webshot)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggalluvial)
library(networkD3)
library(htmlwidgets)
library(TCGAbiolinks)
library(SummarizedExperiment)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(parseMetab)
# remove.packages("parseMetab")
# detach("package:KinaseDownstream", unload = TRUE)
# remotes::install_github("Maj18/parseMetab", dependencies = TRUE)
# library(parseMetab)
```

```{r}
outdir = "../results/CellFieTasksVsKEGGpathways/"
dir.create(outdir, recursive=T, showWarnings=F)
```

# Import data

```{r}
# Import Zhou2020 CellFie task scores
Zhou2020_CellFie = 
    readRDS("../results/CellFie_Zhou2020/CellFieOut/cfs.RDS")
cancer_types = Zhou2020_CellFie %>% 
    pull(Cancer_type) %>% 
    unique()
Zhou2020_CellFie = 
    lapply(cancer_types, function(ct) {
        filter(Zhou2020_CellFie, Cancer_type==ct) %>% 
        dplyr::select(Depth3, TaskScore, Sample) %>% 
        spread(key=Depth3, value=TaskScore) %>%
        tibble::column_to_rownames(var="Sample") %>% 
        t() 
}) %>% setNames(cancer_types)
rm(cancer_types)

# Import Hu2025 CellFie task scores
Hu2025_CellFie = 
    readRDS("../results/CellFie_Hu2025/CellFieOut/cfs.RDS")
cancer_types = Hu2025_CellFie %>% 
    pull(Cancer_type) %>% 
    unique()
Hu2025_CellFie = lapply(cancer_types, function(ct) {
    filter(Hu2025_CellFie, Cancer_type==ct) %>% 
        dplyr::select(Depth3, TaskScore, Sample) %>% 
        spread(key=Depth3, value=TaskScore) %>%
        tibble::column_to_rownames(var="Sample") %>% 
        t() 
}) %>% setNames(cancer_types)
rm(cancer_types)    

# Import Zhou2020 KEGG pathway scores
Zhou2020_GSVA = 
    readRDS("../results/Zhou2020_GSVA/Limma_GSVA/GSVAlimma_rslt_all.RDS") %>%
    lapply(., function(ct) {
        ct$gsva
    })
dim(Zhou2020_GSVA[[1]])

# Import Hu2025 KEGG pathway scores
Hu2025_GSVA = 
    readRDS("../results/Hu2025_GSVA/Limma_GSVA/GSVAlimma_rslt_all.RDS") %>%
    lapply(., function(ct) {
        ct$gsva
})

```

## Import meta data

```{r}
Hu2025_meta = 
    readRDS("../results/CellFie_Hu2025/data/Hu2025/Meta_hu2025.RDS")
Zhou2020_meta = 
    readRDS("../results/CellFie_Zhou2020/data/Zhou2020/meta.RDS")
Hu2025_meta_mapping = 
    setNames(Hu2025_meta$Condition, Hu2025_meta$Sample)
Zhou2020_meta_mapping = 
    setNames(Zhou2020_meta$Condition, Zhou2020_meta$Sample)

```

# Global correlation analysis

```{r}
## Zhou2020
shared_celltypes_Zhou = 
    intersect(names(Zhou2020_CellFie), names(Zhou2020_GSVA))
cor_list_Zhou = lapply(shared_celltypes_Zhou, function(ct) {
    CF_sub = Zhou2020_CellFie[[ct]]
    CSVA_sub = Zhou2020_GSVA[[ct]]
    samples = intersect(colnames(CF_sub), colnames(CSVA_sub))
    normal_samples = 
        names(Zhou2020_meta_mapping)[Zhou2020_meta_mapping=="Normal"] %>%
        intersect(., samples)
    tumor_samples =
        names(Zhou2020_meta_mapping)[Zhou2020_meta_mapping=="Tumor"] %>%
        intersect(., samples)
    correlation_profiles_normal = 
        cor(t(CF_sub[, normal_samples]), t(CSVA_sub[, normal_samples]),
            method="spearman", use="pairwise.complete.obs")
    correlation_profiles_tumor = 
        cor(t(CF_sub[, tumor_samples]), t(CSVA_sub[, tumor_samples]),
            method="spearman", use="pairwise.complete.obs")

    return(list(correlation_profiles_normal=correlation_profiles_normal, 
        correlation_profiles_tumor=correlation_profiles_tumor))
}) %>% setNames(shared_celltypes_Zhou)
cor_list_Zhou_Tumor = lapply(cor_list_Zhou, function(ct){
    ct$correlation_profiles_tumor
})
cor_list_Zhou_Normal = lapply(cor_list_Zhou, function(ct) {
    ct$correlation_profiles_normal
})
sapply(cor_list_Zhou_Tumor, function(ct) dim(ct))
sapply(cor_list_Zhou_Normal, function(ct) dim(ct))
# Make all df with the same rownames and the same column names
cor_list_Zhou_Tumor_standardized = standadizeDFinList(cor_list_Zhou_Tumor)
cor_list_Zhou_Normal_standardized = standadizeDFinList(cor_list_Zhou_Normal)
sapply(cor_list_Zhou_Tumor_standardized, function(ct) dim(ct))
sapply(cor_list_Zhou_Normal_standardized, function(ct) dim(ct))


## Hu2025
shared_celltypes_Hu= 
    intersect(names(Hu2025_CellFie), names(Hu2025_GSVA))
cor_list_Hu = lapply(shared_celltypes_Hu, function(ct) {
    CF_sub = Hu2025_CellFie[[ct]]
    CSVA_sub = Hu2025_GSVA[[ct]]
    samples = intersect(colnames(CF_sub), colnames(CSVA_sub))
    normal_samples = 
        names(Hu2025_meta_mapping)[Hu2025_meta_mapping=="Normal"] %>%
        intersect(., samples)
    tumor_samples =
        names(Hu2025_meta_mapping)[Hu2025_meta_mapping=="Tumor"] %>%
        intersect(., samples)
    correlation_profiles_normal = 
        cor(t(CF_sub[, normal_samples]), t(CSVA_sub[, normal_samples]),
            method="spearman", use="pairwise.complete.obs")
    correlation_profiles_tumor = 
        cor(t(CF_sub[, tumor_samples]), t(CSVA_sub[, tumor_samples]),
            method="spearman", use="pairwise.complete.obs")

    return(list(correlation_profiles_normal=correlation_profiles_normal, 
        correlation_profiles_tumor=correlation_profiles_tumor))
}) %>% setNames(shared_celltypes_Hu)
cor_list_Hu_Tumor = lapply(cor_list_Hu, function(ct){
    ct$correlation_profiles_tumor
})
cor_list_Hu_Normal = lapply(cor_list_Hu, function(ct) {
    ct$correlation_profiles_normal
})
sapply(cor_list_Hu_Tumor, function(ct) dim(ct))
sapply(cor_list_Hu_Normal, function(ct) dim(ct))
# Make all df with the same rownames and the same column names
cor_list_Hu_Tumor_standardized = standadizeDFinList(cor_list_Hu_Tumor)
cor_list_Hu_Normal_standardized = standadizeDFinList(cor_list_Hu_Normal)
sapply(cor_list_Hu_Tumor_standardized, function(ct) dim(ct))
sapply(cor_list_Hu_Normal_standardized, function(ct) dim(ct))

```

### Take median corrlation across all cancer types, and only select those with Rho>0.5

```{r}
getFilteredMedianCor = function(cor_list) {
    array_3d = 
        simplify2array(lapply(cor_list, as.matrix))
    median= 
        apply(array_3d, c(1,2), median, na.rm=TRUE) %>%
        as.data.frame() %>%
        tibble::rownames_to_column(var="CellFie_Task") %>%
        pivot_longer(cols=-CellFie_Task, names_to="KEGG_Pathway", values_to="Rho") %>%
        filter(Rho>0.5)
    return(median)
}

FilteredMedianCor_list_Hu_Tumor = getFilteredMedianCor(cor_list_Hu_Tumor_standardized) 
FilteredMedianCor_list_Hu_Normal = getFilteredMedianCor(cor_list_Hu_Normal_standardized) 
FilteredMedianCor_list_Zhou_Tumor = getFilteredMedianCor(cor_list_Zhou_Tumor_standardized) 
FilteredMedianCor_list_Zhou_Normal = getFilteredMedianCor(cor_list_Zhou_Normal_standardized)
openxlsx::write.xlsx(list(
    Hu2025_Tumor = FilteredMedianCor_list_Hu_Tumor,
    Hu2025_Normal = FilteredMedianCor_list_Hu_Normal,
    Zhou2020_Tumor = FilteredMedianCor_list_Zhou_Tumor,
    Zhou2020_Normal = FilteredMedianCor_list_Zhou_Normal),
    paste0(outdir, "FilteredMedianCor.xlsx"))

```

### Visualize the corresponse between CellFie tasks and KEGG pathways

```{r}
## Import KEGG pathway classes
databaseDIR="../results/TCGA/KEGG_metabolism/"
kegg_metab_db_table = 
    readRDS(paste0(databaseDIR, "/KEGG_metabolism_db_table.RDS")) %>%
    dplyr::select(gs_name, class) %>%
    unique() %>%
    setNames(c("KEGG_Pathway", "Class")) %>%
    mutate(Class = paste0("KEGG_", Class))

## Import CellFie task systems
CellFie_systems = 
    readRDS("../results/CellFie_Zhou2020/CellFieOut/cfs.RDS")  %>%
    dplyr::select(Depth3, Depth1) %>%
    unique() %>%
    setNames(c("CellFie_Task", "System")) %>%
    mutate(System = paste0("CF_", System))

Normal = rbind(FilteredMedianCor_list_Hu_Normal, 
    FilteredMedianCor_list_Zhou_Normal) %>%
    arrange(-Rho) %>% 
    mutate(temp = paste0(CellFie_Task, "_", KEGG_Pathway))
Normal1 = Normal[!duplicated(Normal$CellFie_Task), ] %>% 
    dplyr::select(-temp) 
Normal2 = Normal[!duplicated(Normal$KEGG_Pathway), ] %>% 
    dplyr::select(-temp)
Normal = rbind(Normal1, Normal2) %>% unique %>%
    left_join(kegg_metab_db_table) %>%
    left_join(CellFie_systems) %>%
    mutate(CellFie_Task = paste0("CF_", CellFie_Task)) %>%
    mutate(KEGG_Pathway = paste0("KEGG_", KEGG_Pathway)) %>%
    arrange(Class, KEGG_Pathway)
openxlsx::write.xlsx(list(Normal),
    paste0(outdir, "/FilteredMedianCor_ZhouHuCombined_NormalSamples.xlsx"))

```

```{r}
library(plotly)
library(htmlwidgets)
library(dplyr)
library(RColorBrewer) # Standard palette library, install it if missing

# 1. Gather all unique node names across your four categories
system_nodes   <- unique(as.character(Normal$System))
task_nodes     <- unique(as.character(Normal$CellFie_Task))
pathway_nodes  <- unique(as.character(Normal$KEGG_Pathway))
class_nodes    <- unique(as.character(Normal$Class))

all_node_names <- c(system_nodes, task_nodes, pathway_nodes, class_nodes)

# 2. Build the horizontal constraint coordinates (Columns 1-4)
nodes <- data.frame(
  name = all_node_names,
  x = c(
    rep(0.01, length(system_nodes)),   # Column 1
    rep(0.33, length(task_nodes)),     # Column 2
    rep(0.66, length(pathway_nodes)),  # Column 3
    rep(0.99, length(class_nodes))     # Column 4
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# 3. Create a clean 0-indexed integer lookup map for javascript
mapping <- setNames(seq_len(nrow(nodes)) - 1, nodes$name)

# 4. Define your colors for each unique System node
# Uses standard Set1 qualitative colors, or swaps to a longer palette if needed
num_systems <- length(system_nodes)
system_colors <- brewer.pal(max(3, num_systems), "Set1")[1:num_systems]
names(system_colors) <- system_nodes

# Map colors to the node blocks themselves
node_color_vector <- rep("lightgrey", length(all_node_names))
node_color_vector[1:num_systems] <- system_colors # Color the System rectangles

# 5. Build sequential connections while passing down the origin 'System' tracking column
# Connection Tier 1: System -> CellFie_Task
link_pair1 <- data.frame(
  System_Origin = as.character(Normal$System), # Track who started this path
  Source = as.character(Normal$System),
  Target = as.character(Normal$CellFie_Task),
  Value  = 1,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Connection Tier 2: CellFie_Task -> KEGG_Pathway (Track origin via matching Normal structure)
link_pair2 <- data.frame(
  System_Origin = as.character(Normal$System),
  Source = as.character(Normal$CellFie_Task),
  Target = as.character(Normal$KEGG_Pathway),
  Value  = as.numeric(Normal$Rho),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Connection Tier 3: KEGG_Pathway -> Class
link_pair3 <- data.frame(
  System_Origin = as.character(Normal$System),
  Source = as.character(Normal$KEGG_Pathway),
  Target = as.character(Normal$Class),
  Value  = 1,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# 6. Combine pairs and construct the matching link colors
Link_Normal <- rbind(link_pair1, link_pair2, link_pair3) %>%
  unique() %>%
  mutate(
    Source_Idx = as.numeric(mapping[Source]),
    Target_Idx = as.numeric(mapping[Target])
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Source_Idx) & !is.na(Target_Idx))

# Convert hex colors to translucent RGBA so layered paths can overlap nicely
rgba_mapping <- sapply(system_colors, function(hex) {
  rgb_val <- col2rgb(hex)
  paste0("rgba(", rgb_val[1], ",", rgb_val[2], ",", rgb_val[3], ", 0.4)") # 0.4 sets transparency
})

# Allocate colors to every individual link based on its System_Origin
Link_Normal$Color <- rgba_mapping[Link_Normal$System_Origin]

# 7. Render using the 'snap' algorithm configuration
p <- plot_ly(
  type = "sankey",
  arrangement = "snap", 
  orientation = "h",
  node = list(
    label     = nodes$name,
    x         = nodes$x, 
    color     = node_color_vector, # Colors the starting boxes
    pad       = 100,     
    thickness = 15,      
    line      = list(color = "black", width = 0.2)
  ),
  link = list(
    source = Link_Normal$Source_Idx,
    target = Link_Normal$Target_Idx,
    value  = Link_Normal$Value,
    color  = Link_Normal$Color  # 🌟 CRITICAL LINE: Colors the paths
  )
) %>%
  layout(
    width  = 1500,
    height = 2000, 
    margin = list(l = 40, r = 40, t = 40, b = 40)
  )

# 8. Override the default responsive container layout rules
p$sizingPolicy$browser$fill    <- FALSE
p$sizingPolicy$browser$padding <- 0
p$sizingPolicy$viewer$suppress <- TRUE

# Save the final file
saveWidget(p, file = paste0(outdir, "Task_pathway_sankey.html"), selfcontained = TRUE)

```

### Summarize and Visualize the corresponse between CellFie tasks and KEGG pathways to System and Class levels

```{r}
systemClass = Normal %>% dplyr::select(System, Class) %>%
    dplyr::count(System, Class, name="Value")
library(plotly)
library(htmlwidgets)
library(dplyr)
library(RColorBrewer)

# 1. Isolate unique nodes across your two columns
system_nodes <- unique(as.character(systemClass$System))
class_nodes  <- unique(as.character(systemClass$Class))
all_node_names <- c(system_nodes, class_nodes)

# 2. Bundle into a clean nodes data frame
nodes <- data.frame(name = all_node_names, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# 3. Create a 0-indexed reference map for JavaScript conversion
mapping <- setNames(seq_len(nrow(nodes)) - 1, nodes$name)

# 4. Handle System-level color coding
num_systems <- length(system_nodes)
# Use 'Set3' or 'Paired' if you have more than 8-9 systems
system_colors <- brewer.pal(max(3, num_systems), "Set1")[1:num_systems]
names(system_colors) <- system_nodes

# Color starting node bars dynamically, keep destination bars light grey
node_color_vector <- rep("lightgrey", length(all_node_names))
node_color_vector[1:num_systems] <- system_colors

# Convert hex values to translucent RGBA strings (0.4 sets the path transparency)
rgba_mapping <- sapply(system_colors, function(hex) {
  rgb_val <- col2rgb(hex)
  paste0("rgba(", rgb_val[1,1], ",", rgb_val[2,1], ",", rgb_val[3,1], ", 0.4)")
})

# 5. Build your clean 0-indexed connections table
Link_Data <- systemClass %>%
  mutate(
    Source_Idx = as.numeric(mapping[System]),
    Target_Idx = as.numeric(mapping[Class]),
    Color      = rgba_mapping[System]
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Source_Idx) & !is.na(Target_Idx))

# 6. Generate the interactive Plotly widget canvas
p <- plot_ly(
  type = "sankey",
  orientation = "h",
  node = list(
    label     = nodes$name,
    color     = node_color_vector,
    pad       = 30,         # Visual padding separating node stack rows
    thickness = 20,         # Physical width of the category block rectangles
    line      = list(color = "black", width = 0.2)
  ),
  link = list(
    source = Link_Data$Source_Idx,
    target = Link_Data$Target_Idx,
    value  = Link_Data$Value,
    color  = Link_Data$Color  # Links display matching translucent system colors
  )
) %>%
  layout(
    width  = 625,          # Comfortable size for a dual-column flow layout
    height = 400,    
    margin = list(l = 30, r = 30, t = 40, b = 30)
  )

# 7. Protect layout tracking constraints against screen-distortion
p$sizingPolicy$browser$fill    <- FALSE
p$sizingPolicy$browser$padding <- 0
p$sizingPolicy$viewer$suppress <- TRUE

# Save the final file locally
saveWidget(p, file = paste0(outdir, "/system_class_flow.html"), selfcontained = TRUE)

```


```{r}
save.image(paste0(outdir, "/TCGA.RData"))
# load(paste0(outdir, "/TCGA.RData"))
out = capture.output(sessionInfo())
writeLines(out, paste0(outdir, "/sessionInfo.txt"))
```
